name: generating-rbs
description: Generates RBS type signatures from Ruby source files. Triggers when creating type definitions, adding types to Ruby code, or generating .rbs files for classes/modules without existing RBS signatures.
capabilities: Analyzes Ruby source files to identify classes, modules, methods, and constants
Generates pure RBS signature files following RBS syntax conventions
Eliminates untyped types by inferring proper types from code context
Validates RBS signatures using rbs validate command
Checks type safety with steep check when Steep is configured
author: DmitryPogrebnoy
securitySummary: Static findings (1979) are false positives. The scanner misinterprets reference documentation files as executable code. All external_commands findings are markdown code blocks, blocker findings are RBS type signatures, and network findings are documentation links. The skill only reads Ruby files and generates RBS signatures - no network calls, credential access, or dangerous capabilities exist.
valueStatement: This skill generates RBS type signatures for Ruby code from scratch. It analyzes Ruby source files and creates corresponding .rbs signature files with proper type definitions.
userTitle: Generate RBS Type Signatures for Ruby
limitations: Does not handle RBS-inline signatures or update existing signatures
Does not execute Ruby code from the project
Requires user clarification for ambiguous type scenarios
Only performs type safety checks if Steep is configured in the project

useCases.title: Add Static Types to Ruby Codebase
useCases.description: Generate type signatures for Ruby classes without existing RBS files

useCases.title: Prepare Types for Type Checking
useCases.description: Create RBS files needed for Steep static type checking

useCases.title: Document Public API Types
useCases.description: Generate type signatures for library classes and modules

promptTemplates.title: Basic RBS Generation
promptTemplates.scenario: Generate types for a single class
promptTemplates.prompt: Generate RBS signatures for app/models/user.rb and place them in the sig/ directory

promptTemplates.title: Module with Methods
promptTemplates.scenario: Generate types for a module with multiple methods
promptTemplates.prompt: Create RBS signatures for lib/services/payment_processor.rb including all public and private methods

promptTemplates.title: Generic Classes
promptTemplates.prompt: Generate RBS signatures for lib/collections/repository.rb with proper type parameters for generics

promptTemplates.title: Full Project Types
promptTemplates.scenario: Generate types for entire project structure
promptTemplates.prompt: Generate RBS signatures for all Ruby files in app/ and lib/ directories, maintaining directory structure in sig/

bestPractices: Run rbs validate after generating signatures to ensure internal consistency
Use steep check to verify type safety when Steep is configured
Review generated signatures for unnecessary untyped types
antiPatterns: Using untyped as a default instead of inferring proper types
Generating signatures without reviewing for correctness
Skipping validation with rbs validate before committing

faq.question: What is the difference between this skill and RBS inline?
faq.answer: This skill generates standalone .rbs files. RBS inline generates comments within Ruby files.

faq.question: Does this skill work with RBS inline annotations?
faq.answer: No, this skill only generates pure RBS signatures from scratch, not from inline annotations.

faq.question: What Ruby syntax does this skill support?
faq.answer: Full Ruby syntax including generics, blocks, keyword arguments, and union types.

faq.question: Where should generated RBS files be placed?
faq.answer: In the sig/ directory, mirroring the Ruby source file directory structure.

faq.question: Can this skill update existing RBS signatures?
faq.answer: No, this skill only generates new signatures from scratch. Use other tools for updates.

faq.question: What happens if a method has untyped parameters?
faq.answer: The skill attempts to infer proper types from code context. If inference fails, untyped is used as last resort.
